observations.recommendation: We recommended that all unpaid perfected obligations be recorded as required by Section 4 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting Systems (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
9. Maintain RPTAR or the duly certified RPTAR/Taxpayer’s list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the current year as the basis for recording the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts in compliance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and
10. Submit the Certified List of RPT Delinquencies as of December 31, 2023, that reconciles with the RPT and SET Receivable balances.
11. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare a JEV to correct the RPT/SET Receivable based on the updated RPTAR provided by MTO, maintain RPT and SET Receivables SLs, and reconcile the accounts with the Municipal Treasurer’s records.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the accounting and reporting guidelines outlined in COA Circular No. 2015-008. The following actions should be taken:
24. The Inventory Committee should conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all local road networks within the Municipality's jurisdiction and reconcile the inventory with the records maintained by the accounting office, if available.
25. The Municipal Engineer should collaborate with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to provide a complete description and cost breakdown of road components for all local road networks. This collaboration should involve coordinating with the Municipal Assessor to assess the road lot components accurately.
26. The Municipal Treasurer should establish and maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map. A dedicated Local Road Network Property Card should be maintained for each road network, documenting relevant information.
27. The Municipal Accountant should ensure that all local road networks and their respective components are recorded in the municipality's book of accounts. Depreciation expenses for each component should also be accounted for.
28. The Municipal Accountant should include a disclosure of the total road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to undertake a thorough review and analysis of the recorded A/Ps amounting to ₱3,517,678.80 as of December 31, 2022, and recommend for reversion all those which remained outstanding for two years or more, and which no actual claims, administrative or judicial, have been filed or which are not covered by perfected contracts on record in accordance with Section 98 of P.D. No. 1445 and DBM-COA Joint Circular No. 99-06 dated November 13, 1999.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct all department heads concerned to submit their respective duly accomplished AAPSI and assign personnel to consolidate the same for submission to the Auditor at the soonest possible time and within 60 days upon receipt of the AARs for the succeeding years. 32. We further recommended that pertinent documents to support the Actions Taken should also be submitted to facilitate our validation of the responses.

We recommended that the Management:
35. Require the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Engineer to prioritize the proper accounting and inventory of the land parcels owned by the Municipality.
36. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a Land Subsidiary Ledger or Schedule of Details that would reconcile with the GL amount.
37. Take appropriate action to have all land belonging to the municipality be covered by the TCT duly issued by the LRA to substantiate its claim of ownership; and
38. Require the production of titles and be turned over to the Municipal Treasurer for proper custody and safekeeping in the absence of a property custodian.

We reiterated our audit recommendations that Management to (a) require all concerned accountable officers to immediately settle their cash advances in accordance with the pertinent sections of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; 40. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular evaluation/validation of all outstanding accounts; send demand letters to officers and employees with long outstanding accounts, and impose sanctions when necessary, such as withholding of their salaries as provided in the Circular; and if warranted, request for the write-off of long-overdue cash advances in accordance with the procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer determine the proper corrections to be made and thereafter, Management require the contractor to effect the necessary corrections as soon as possible to avoid further deterioration. We further recommend that, henceforth, require the Municipal Engineer to continuously supervise and monitor during the implementation of projects to ensure good quality workmanship.

Required the Municipal Treasurer to send bills of collection to the identifiable and existing debtors.
44. Request for write-off of receivables found uncollectible from COA Central Office to clear the financial statements of accounts of doubtful collectability.
Direct all concerned Municipal employees to settle their audit disallowances as required under COA Circular No. 2009-006 to enable the Municipality to utilize the funds for their priority projects.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer in coordination with the Punong Barangays to submit copies of duly signed distribution lists showing the full names of recipients as well as the date of receipt and such other necessary information including the quantity of food items received in order to validate actual distribution and to establish accountability.

We recommended that the Management create an inventory committee to conduct a physical count of the breeding stock. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Agriculturist and Municipal Treasurer in the retrieval of available records to trace the costs of the Breeding Stocks.

We recommended that Management submit a Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances supported with (a) Proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries and (b) Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, in accordance with Section 1 of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
Required the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to return the balances of all PDAF/CDF to the Bureau of Treasury.

Consider transferring the deposits maintained with First Consolidated Bank to authorized government depository banks, to eliminate exposure of government funds from certain risks particularly on insolvency and bank closure.

1.12 We recommended that Management observe the guidelines and procedures
provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, for the one-
time cleansing of PPE account balances to ensure the reliability and
verifiability of PPE balances in terms of existence, condition, and
accountability as follows:

a. Submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed
period;
b. Adopt the uniform numbering system of PPE and require the Property
Unit to update the property stickers based on the prescribed format and to
include the required information under Section 5.6 and 5.7 of COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;
c. Temporarily relieve the members of the Inventory Committee from their
regular duties to allow them to devote their time and attention to the
conduct of the physical inventory taking; and
d. Strictly comply with the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of
physical count of PPE, recognition of PPE items found at the station, and
disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items. Additionally, require the
Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to reconcile their records
based on the results of the actual physical inventory and make the
necessary adjustments accordingly.

2.17 We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the accounting and
reporting guidelines outlined in COA Circular No. 2015-008. The following
actions should be taken:

a. The Inventory Committee conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all
local road networks within the Municipality's jurisdiction and reconcile the
inventory with the records maintained by the accounting office, if available.
b. The Municipal Engineer collaborate with the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to provide a complete description and cost breakdown
of road components for all local road networks, coordinating with the
Municipal Assessor to accurately assess the road lot components.
c. The Municipal Treasurer establish and maintain a Local Road Inventory,
Road Map, and Local Road Network Property Card for each road network,
documenting relevant information.
d. The Municipal Accountant ensure that all local road networks and their
respective components are recorded in the municipality's books of accounts,
with depreciation expenses for each component also be accounted for.
e. The Municipal Accountant include a disclosure of the total road network
system in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the Municipality.

3.17 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer coordinate with and provide
the Municipal Accountant, at least on a quarterly basis, with copies of the
Accomplishment Reports and the Certificates of Completion and Acceptance
for completed projects to facilitate the timely and accurate recording of
completed infrastructure in the books of accounts.

3.18 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify the balances of
the CIP accounts and, upon validation, prepare the necessary Journal Entry
Vouchers to close the relevant CIP accounts and transfer the costs to the
appropriate Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) asset account.
Additionally, compute and record the corresponding depreciation expenses to
ensure accurate and reliable PPE balances in the financial statements.

3.19 Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from
recording CIP entries based on the approved contracts but rather on the actual
delivery of services/accomplishment.

4.7 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer:
1. Rigorously evaluate the readiness and feasibility of projects before
including them in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP);
2. Ensure that only projects with secured requirements, such as land
availability, RROW, and finalized plans, are programmed for
implementation;
3. Closely monitor the procurement process and address bottlenecks
promptly to avoid delays; and
4. Strengthen coordination among relevant offices to align project planning,
budgeting, and implementation with the approved timeline.

5.7 We recommended that the LDRRM Council pass a resolution authorizing the
reversion to the Special Trust Fund of the unutilized LDRRMF amounts that are
still within the five-year holding period, and to identify disaster related programs,
projects, and activities to be funded therefrom for incorporation in the
Municipality’s LDRRMFIP, in conformity with Section 5.1.13 of the COA
Circular No. 2012-002;

5.8 Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the
necessary journal entry vouchers to transfer the said unutilized LDRRMF
back to the Special Trust Fund.

6.7 We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal
Accountant, promptly remit all taxes withheld from employees’
compensation, procurements, and documentary stamps in accordance with
the prescribed period outlined in BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular
(RMC) No. 23-2012, dated February 14, 2012. We further recommended and
the Municipal Accountant agreed to maintain the subsidiary ledgers for each
specific account.

7.11 We recommended and Management agreed, to implement a review process
that would ensure that the monthly remittance matches the total funds
withheld, including the performance of reconciliation procedures to address
any discrepancies between the withheld amounts and the prepared
remittances, and thereafter ensure the timely remittance of all withheld funds
in compliance with the applicable laws, rules, and regulations governing the
remittances to GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-ibig.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to take up the necessary adjusting/correcting entries in the books on the advance payments of RPT and SET collected in CY 2023.
13. We further recommended that the MTO indicate in the report of collections, advance payments with the corresponding taxable year, as a basis for the accounting office in making correct accounting entries, regarding collections of RPT and SET to reflect the correct amount of income, receivables, and other affected accounts in the FS.

We recommended that Management collect from E.D. Angas Construction the excess contract cost over the cost per COA Technical Evaluation amounting to ₱793,865.08, and further recommended that, henceforth, the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group conduct a thorough evaluation of the detailed cost estimates of the contract to avoid incurring excessive expenditures.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council pass a resolution reverting the unutilized funds that have already lapsed the five-year validity period to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for adoption by the Sangguniang Bayan through the Local Development Council to be made available for other social services, in conformity with Section 5.1.13 of the COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
21. Ensure that any savings from the completed projects of LDRRMF-CO are retained in the General Fund and made available for use in disaster risk reduction and management activities.
22. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make a journal entry to transfer the savings back to the General Fund following COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated December 19, 2016.

We further recommended that the LDRRM Council pass a resolution reverting the unutilized funds that have already lapsed the five-year validity period to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for adoption by the Sangguniang Bayan through the Local Development Council to be made available for other social services, in conformity with Section 5.1.13 of the Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council: 29. Pass a resolution reverting the unutilized funds that have already lapsed five years to the General Fund for adoption by the Sangguniang Bayan through the Local Development Council to be made available for other social services, in conformity with Section 5.1.13 of the Circular No. 2012-002; and 30. Ensure that any savings from the completed projects of LDRRMF-CO are retained in the General Fund and made available for use in disaster risk reduction and management activities. 31. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a journal entry to transfer the savings back to the General Fund following COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned LGU official to submit within the reglementary period the approved copy of the GAD Plan and Budget and ensure that the annual accomplishment report is submitted on time.
Organize immediately an Internal Audit Service (IAS) to comply with Section 4 of AO No. 70 to assist the municipality in the discharge of its functions.

This collaboration should involve coordinating with the Municipal Assessor to assess the road lot components accurately.

5. The Municipal Accountant should include a disclosure of the total road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
1.15. We recommended and the Management agreed to observe the guidelines and
procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020,
for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to have reliable PPE
balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability as
follows:

a. Issue an Office Order to create/reconstitute the Inventory Committee, as
some have already retired from service;
b. Submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed
period;
c. Adopt the uniform numbering system for property numbers of PPE and
require the Property Unit to update the property stickers based on the
prescribed format and to include the required information under Section
5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
d. Strictly follow the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical
count of PPE, recognition of PPE items found at the station, and disposition
for non-existing/missing PPE items and require the Accountant and
Property Officer to reconcile their records based on the results of the
actual physical inventory and effect necessary adjustments.

2.16. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

a. Maintain the SLs for the Due to NGAs account duly indicating the pertinent
information such as the date when the fund was received, the source agency,
the purpose, and the amount received, utilized, and liquidated;
b. Ensure that fund transfers recorded under the Trust Fund are duly
supported by a MOA; and
c. Return the excess funds for the fully implemented/completed projects
amounting to ₱431,908.13 to the respective Source Agencies and the PDAF
amounting to ₱2,588,266.70 to the Bureau of Treasury.

3.9. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed that all unpaid
perfected obligations be recorded under the Accounts Payable account to come
up with reliable balances of accounts in the financial statements before closing
the books of accounts at the end of each calendar year.

4.14. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare a JEV to correct
the RPT/SET Receivable based on the updated RPTAR provided by MTO,
maintain RPT and SET Receivables SLs, and reconcile the accounts with the
Municipal Treasurer’s records.

5.8. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to take up the
necessary adjusting/correcting entries in the books on the advance payments of
RPT and SET collected in CY 2023.

5.9. We further recommended that the MTO indicate in the report of collections,
advance payments with the corresponding taxable year, as a basis for the
accounting office in making correct accounting entries, regarding collections of
RPT and SET to reflect the correct amount of income, receivables, and other
affected accounts in the FS.

6.6. We recommended that Management collect from E.D. Angas Construction the
excess contract cost over the cost per COA technical evaluation amounting to
₱794,865.08.

6.7. We further recommended that, henceforth, the Bids and Awards Committee
and the Technical Working Group conduct a thorough evaluation of the detailed
cost estimates of the contract to avoid incurring excessive expenditures.

7.11. We recommended that Management:

a. Expedite the process of hiring and filling the position of Municipal
Assessor;
b. Coordinate with and seek technical assistance from the Provincial Assessor
and/or the DOF-Bureau of Local Government Finance to fast-track the
preparation of the updated SMVs of the Municipality and cause the drafting
of an Ordinance for consideration of the Sanggunian, incorporating therein
the proposed SMV, assessment level and tax rate to support the conduct of
GRRPA within the reglementary period;
c. Augment the number of qualified personnel assigned at the Municipal
Assessor’s Office to meet the minimum personnel requirements stipulated
in the MRPAAO and conduct capacity-building interventions to facilitate
the conduct of the general revision of real property assessments in
adherence to Section 219 of the same Code and DILG-DOF JMC No. 2010-
001, and closely monitor its completion; and
d. Make representation with the Local Chief Executive/Local Sanggunian to
appropriate funds for the computerization of the assessment records to
speed up the completion of GRRPA data gathering and encoding.

8.7. We recommended that the LDRRM Council:

a) Pass a resolution reverting the unutilized funds that have already lapsed five
years to the General Fund unappropriated surplus for adoption by the
Sangguniang Bayan through the Local Development Council to be made
available for other social services, in conformity with Section 5.1.13 of the
COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
b) Ensure that any savings from the completed projects of LDRRMF-CO are
retained in the General Fund and made available for use in disaster risk
reduction and management activities.

8.8. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make a
journal entry to transfer the savings back to the General Fund following COA
Circular No. 2012-002.

9.6. We recommended that the MDRRMO coordinate with the MDRRMC in
identifying the PPAs to be charged to the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF
balance for inclusion in the LDRRMFIP in accordance with Section 5.1.2 of
COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

6. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, the unexpended DRRM transferred from GF to the STF, and other funds received from grants and donations for LDRRM in accordance with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal DRRM Officer properly identify and assess the hazards susceptibility and the related risk that may occur in the Municipality in order to formulate a feasible LDRRMFIP.
We further recommended that the Municipal DRRM Officer strive for the timely and full implementation of LDRRM-related PPAs to achieve the goals and objectives of the fund as well as the desired benefits and strictly observe the provisions of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2013-1 in allocating LDRRM Fund solely for the implementation of DRRM-related projects.

14. We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, when preparing the POW for infrastructure projects, strictly observe the provisions of DPWH DO 197 dated October 7, 2016, to prevent the overstatement of the ABC and ensure more accurate cost estimations.
15. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor exhaust all available legal remedies provided under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to impose appropriate sanctions on the erring contractor, emphasizing the importance of fulfilling the contractual obligations and completing the project in a timely manner.

cash advances in accordance with the pertinent sections of COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997;

liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period, and see to it
that no additional cash advance shall be allowed unless the previous ones
are settled or properly accounted for; and
(c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular evaluation/
validation of all outstanding accounts; send demand letters to officers
and employees with long outstanding accounts, and impose sanctions
when necessary, such as withholding of their salaries as provided in the
Circular; and if warranted, request for the write-off of long-overdue
cash advances in accordance with the procedures under COA Circular
No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a) Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of
inventories and movable properties annually;
b) Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the report on the results of
the physical count using the prescribed form and reconcile the results
with the accounting records; and
c) Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain
the supplies/PPE ledger cards and stock/property cards, respectively.

We recommended that Management:
a) Require the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal
Engineer to prioritize the proper accounting and inventory of the land
parcels owned by the Municipality;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a Land Subsidiary
Ledger or Schedule of Details that would reconcile with the GL
amount;
c) Take appropriate action to have all land belonging to the municipality
be covered by the TCT duly issued by the LRA to substantiate its claim
of ownership; and
d) Require the production of titles and be turned over to the Municipal
Treasurer for proper custody and safekeeping in the absence of a
property custodian.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineer to a)
conduct inspection to determine the actual accomplishment of the contractor less
the portion of the old structure utilized and b) submit as built plan taking into
account the costs of the existing structure utilized as well as a justification as to the
structural strength of the portion of the undemolished old structure.
In the event that the structural integrity of the infrastructure asset is
compromised, we recommend that Management enforce the termination of contract

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management create the Inventory Committee to conduct a
complete physical inventory of all the properties of the municipality to establish the
actual existence of these assets as basis for the Municipal Accountant to reconcile its
records and to effect the necessary adjustments in the books and to submit the
inventory report to COA as soon as possible.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant hasten the preparation of the CY
2016 financial reports and submit these to COA at the soonest possible time. We
further recommended that, henceforth, Management closely monitor compliance on
the submission of monthly and quarterly financial reports with complete schedules
in accordance with the requirement of pertinent laws and regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in digging up records to establish the
names of employees/establishments these taxes were deducted/withheld and to remit
immediately to the BIR all withheld taxes of prior years and for taxes that shall be
withheld thereafter on or before the 10th day of the month following the month when
the withholding was made as required under Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to send
notices to the payees of the stale checks and to submit the original copies of the
cancelled checks. Likewise, direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal
entry voucher to take up the cancellation of the stale checks. Henceforth, strictly
observe Section 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs on proper treatment and
accounting procedures for stale and cancelled checks.

We recommend that the Letter/Certificate of Acceptance shall be issued by the
Municipal Mayor after the one (1) year defects liability period and after all defects
and failures, if any, shall be repaired by the Contractor and inspected by the
Municipal Engineer which in turn shall prepare the Acceptance Report.

We recommend that Management explain the reason for the delay/non-
implementation of the projects and expedite its implementation. Further, we
recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer/Project
Implementor to implement the approved projects identified for the fund release.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Chairman of the Local GAD Council request for the appropriation of funds to finance GAD related projects. He should initiate the implementation of GAD Programs for the materialization of the same which are geared towards the attainment of goals and objectives as mandated by Executive Order No. 273 and DBM/NEDA/NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-001.
observations.managementAction: Only the amount of P500,000.00 was appropriated for GAD. However, the LGU has enough funded GAD activities and programs under the different departments.
observations.reason: Review of the Annual Budget of the Municipality showed that a meager amount of P500,000.00 was appropriated for GAD; however, verification of programs/activities under the different departments showed that some of these programs/activities are GAD related and were implemented to address gender issues.

observations.recommendation: Direct all those who received the PEI to refund the amount of P10,000.00 each, the payment of which is not legally due them. Henceforth, strictly observe the fifty-five percent (55%) limitation on personal services to prevent disallowances in audit. Henceforth, observe strictly the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and prescribed Travel Law to preclude disallowances in audit. Refrain from clearing any official or employee from money accountability if he/she still has an unliquidated cash advance with the government.
observations.managementAction: Partial payments were made through payroll deduction.
observations.reason: Post-audit of payrolls showed minimal repayments of the disallowance.

observations.recommendation: Direct all concerned Municipal employees to settle their audit disallowances as required under COA Circular No. 2009-006 to enable the Municipality to utilize the funds for their priority projects.
observations.managementAction: Partial payments were made through payroll deduction.
observations.reason: Post-audit of payrolls showed minimal repayments of the disallowance.

observations.recommendation: Require the municipal treasurer to send bills of collection to the identifiable and existing debtors; Request for write-off of receivables found uncollectible from COA Central Office to clear the financial statements of accounts of doubtful collectability.
observations.managementAction: Collection letters were sent to identifiable and existing debtors.
observations.reason: Sending of collection letters were not issued periodically.

observations.recommendation: Require the LDRRMO to submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) and other required reports on the use of the fund promptly.
observations.managementAction: There was no submission of the LDRRMFIP and other reports to the Office of the Auditor. However, utilization report of the LDRRM fund was not submitted.
observations.reason: The LDRRMFIP was prepared and given to the Auditor only upon demand.
